title: I Will Teach You to Be Rich

chapters.title: You can get wealthy by changing your financial habits
chapters.text:
Believe it or not, it's not uncommon to see people gain weight after college. Many experts recommend eating less and exercising more to manage weight gain. Where are we going with this? Well, food and money are similar because they compound. Just as people don't put on weight overnight, they don't become rich in a day either. It accumulates little by little until the results become visible.

So, why do most people struggle for financial stability or even freedom? Many have one of these bad money habits: they ignore their finances or keep complaining and don't attempt to leave their economic abyss. These are both surefire ways to remain poor or, at best, average.

Investments do not have to be a do-or-die situation. You can research the financial market to discover and invest in solid stocks. Allow your interest to compound and focus on other things. Furthermore, beware of financial gurus who go on and on about the hottest stocks in the market. If you listen to them, you may lose majorly.

Now more than ever, it is harder to make financial decisions because of the information overload around us.

Just like you don't have to have a degree in nutrition to eat healthily, you also do not need certification in personal finance to cut down your expenses and make plans for your future. Nevertheless, you should deepen your knowledge in an area if you are to succeed in it. Everything starts and ends with you. So, before contributing your money to anything, invest in your knowledge first. Stay up-to-date with all the investing options and restrictions in your area, and never limit yourself to one source of information alone.

In this summary, you'll learn practical ways to build sustainable wealth by avoiding risks and losses. Are you interested? Let's get started!

chapters.title: Beat the credit card companies at their own game
chapters.text:
Modern banking systems can save you money if you negotiate and get yourself the best deal with multiple perks and bonuses like discounts, free car insurance, or flight miles. Credit cards can be tricky, but you should never let them intimidate you. The media is responsible for people's fear of credit cards since it highlights the most dramatic and negative stories. The stats, headlines, and emotions surrounding debts and credit cards scare many people, preventing them from enjoying the advantages of our time.

For those who delay their fees or cannot control their expenses, credit cards become an evil that sucks up all their money. But you should look at a credit card as a fire. Once, people were afraid of fire. However, over time, they found the vital advantages that made their life more comfortable. Unfortunately, banking remains a gray area for many users. Hence, instead of avoiding credit cards, we must learn more about them to discover the magnificent benefits they bring.

To manage credit cards, pay your bills on time and don’t spend over your limit.

Wise credit card users are confident in the possible charges or fees because they find the golden middle ground in their banking experience. Here are some of the commandments they follow:

• Avoid late payments at all costs.
• Try to get discounts.
• Lower your annual percentage rate (APR).
• Stick to your primary credit cards for an extended period.
• Don’t open cards at retail stores.
• Get as many cards as you need for comfort and convenience, but make sure you use them.
• Get more credit to improve your credit utilization rate.
• Use your credit card’s secret perks like trip-cancellation insurance and concierge services.

Credit cards can help you track your spending. Put all your purchases on key credit cards, and you'll find it easier to categorize your expenses. Online banking helps you prevent wasting your resources, so you can save up instead.

The main reason for credit card complaints is the debt many accumulate. To clear it, calculate how much you owe, pay off crucial ones first, seek a lower APR, and figure out additional sources of income.

Hence, ensure you get your credit score and report, set up your credit card, and handle it effectively.

chapters.title: Negotiate fees for higher profits
chapters.text:
Having organized your credit cards, it's time you set up your bank accounts. Banks are the backbone of your financial infrastructure. Therefore, you need to carefully pick the right ones, optimize them, and cut off unnecessary fees. A rule of thumb in selecting the right banks is to check what extra fees apply and what services they offer.

Beyond interest from loans, banks make a lot of money from overdraft fees, and they won't warn or protect you. Unfortunately, many people stick with the banks that drain their money, offering tons of products and increasing the charges. The reason is that many are unaware that there are better options, or find it too troubling to switch banks. Learning the basic information about accounts helps to protect your money.

Unnecessary fees are usually attached to checking accounts, where most people's income arrives. From there, the money transfers to saving or investing accounts. Savings accounts are where you put your short to mid-term savings for goals like a vacation, rent, or an emergency fund. And here's a psychological trick that works: keep your checking account at one bank for everyday expenses, and your savings at a completely different online bank. When you want to impulse-buy that $200 jacket, the two-to-three-day transfer time gives you enough pause to realize you don't actually need it.

Even if you aren't rich yet, setting up your accounts this way enables you to develop effective financial habits. If you don't start when the stakes are low, you won't manage things well when the stakes are high.

When choosing your bank(s), look for values like trust, convenience, and reputation.

Remember to negotiate everything. Avoid monthly fees by calling your bank to ask for a no-fee account. Banks spend $300-500 to acquire each new customer, often waiving fees to keep you.

So, set up your online checking and savings accounts and save bit by bit. Log in to your bank account and write down every fee you paid last month. Circle the ones over $10 — these are your negotiation targets for tomorrow.

Did you know? Forbes revealed that in 2017, banks made more than $34 billion from overdraft fees alone.

chapters.title: Don’t ignore investments
chapters.text:
After getting your financial infrastructure up and running, the next line of action is to open an investment account. If you save $500 monthly in a regular savings account at 0.5% interest, you'll have $61,677 after 10 years. But if you invest that same $500 monthly in the stock market's historical 8% average return, you'll have $82,070. That's $20,393 more for moving your money to the right place. As most financial gurus say, the only way to multiply your wealth is to make money work for you, not the other way around.

Life often throws a curveball and prevents increased income. In this situation, most people will never be wealthy simply because they have the wrong money-related attitudes and behaviors. Think about your friend group: there's probably someone who always talks about "getting serious about money someday" but still hasn't opened an investment account after three years of talking. Don't be that person. The difference between starting to invest at 25 versus 35 is literally hundreds of thousands of dollars by retirement.

Research shows that most older people wish they saved money and invested. They can still do it, but it only gets more challenging with age. Investment is not easy or obvious. It takes time and knowledge to develop this financial habit and become active. Yet, many young people put it off and hope for an inheritance or a lottery win to transform their financial state.

Investments allow you to escape inflation and give you incredible results.

Nonetheless, a safe investment option available in many countries is a retirement savings account — 401(k) in the US. This account has immense advantages for those who spend a certain amount of money. However, you can only withdraw your money after a certain age; otherwise, you'll face severe penalties and money losses. Its purpose is to prevent you from pulling out your money before retirement.

The truth is, you can choose what your Rich Life is and how you get there. ~ Ramit Sethi

Hence, investing is the only way to grow your wealth. You can start with a small amount, but on a weekly or monthly basis, you'll notice your habits change. You'll spend more reasonably and enjoy saving and investing.

chapters.title: You can save up and still buy the things you love
chapters.text:
So far, you have learned about your investment options. It's now time to take control of your expenses. Developing a strategic spending plan is the most complex part of financial management since no one likes to limit their needs and pleasures. However, with some work, you’ll notice that you don’t lose or miss out on anything and manage to save more than expected.

The critical thing you should know to alter your financial situation is to decide who you are: a conscious spender or, as Ramit Sethi states, a "cheap person." A conscious spender says no to the things that do not add value to them and buys what they love.

On the other hand, others try to live up to societal expectations and allow their friends to influence them. They waste the money they don't have on things they don't need to impress people they don't like. "Cheap spenders" think short-term, while conscious people keep long-term values in their minds.

Conscious spending does not mean buying only food or clothes in second-hand stores. Instead, it assumes that you know what you will buy and don't feel guilty.

If you see your financial plan as self-deprivation, it may be challenging to follow.

Classifying all your spending, especially rent, food, and other bills, takes time and effort. Nevertheless, it is vital to be aware of all your purchases, and people rarely remember the payments that occur automatically. The A La Carte Method calls on you to cancel subscriptions and pay for services only when needed. For example, instead of paying for Netflix year-round, cancel it and resubscribe for two months when your favorite shows drop new seasons. You watch the same content but spend around $32 instead of $192 a year subscription — that's $160 saved, enough for a nice dinner out every month. With this method, you:

• Are aware of your expenses every time you pay for things.
• Notice and value your purchase.
• Save 60-80% on subscription services you barely use.

Try this 30-day challenge:

1. Check your last three credit card statements and highlight every recurring subscription.
2. Cancel everything except your top two most-used services.
3. For one month, pay for services only when you actually want to use them.
4. Compare the numbers — most people save $150-300 in the first month alone.

chapters.title: Automate your accounts and save while sleeping
chapters.text:
Automating your money is the single most profitable system you will ever build. While some people play defense by saving more, you can play offense by creating a system using technology to ensure you're still growing your money. Such an approach acknowledges human behavior when we get tired or unmotivated and prevents fines, late fees, and excessive spending.

You can spend as little as 90 minutes a month managing your money using a concept called the “Next $100.” It is about deciding where the next $100 goes — savings or investments.

If you are a conscious spender, you know how much money goes to each area of your life. However, if you keep tracking and managing it every week, you'll get frustrated and forget to pay your bills. Hence, by linking your accounts, the system will transfer a certain amount of your money to each. For example, your salary goes to your checking account and gets split; 20% of it may go automatically to your savings account, and 10% to investments.

Such a method helps you enjoy life without overthinking and managing your financial situation daily. It also helps to avoid simple human mistakes and keeps the tiresome process of payments off your shoulders.

Furthermore, by making technology work for you for free, you don't have to pay someone to do the same job. Many hire an accountant to manage their money, analyze the expenses, and pay the bills. Others pay financial experts to consult on investment options. In reality, many benefit more by contributing to passive funds than by trying to pick expensive fund managers who claim to be able to beat the markets.

If you do the necessary research, you can manage your funds independently without external fund managers.

Unfortunately, many give in to manipulation and risk their savings for the mere game of a broker who may not know more than they do. If you do this, you'll pay a lot for their services, and it won’t guarantee a win or financial safety.

This new information can be off-putting or even frightening at first.

Fear is no excuse to do nothing with your money. When others are scared, there are bargains to be found. ~ Ramit Sethi

Thus, learn to rely on your knowledge and experience. If you lack either of these, invest in them instead of paying a financial expert.

chapters.title: You don’t have to be rich to invest
chapters.text:
Most people like to spend their money instead of managing it. Automatic investing is the perfect option for people who don’t want to waste time but want to receive profits. It involves carefully deciding how to distribute your money across your portfolio. You also need to pick specific investments and automate your regular contributions so you can relax while your money keeps growing.

Automatic investments charge less and promise superior performance.

When people think about investing, they usually imagine brokers who work with hedge funds and stocks shouting into old phone receivers. Although many people keep trading on the market, such an investment is risky and rarely pays off. Moreover, it takes a lot of time and drains your money for no reason. Automatic assets demand less time and effort. Eventually, such tactics will bring you to the point when your investments cover your expenses automatically. Then, you don't have to work anymore. The acronym F.I.R.E. captures it best. It means Financially Independent, Retiring Early.

However, there are other ways of investing, like stocks, shares, bonds, and index funds. So, here's your beginner portfolio that beats 90% of complex strategies: 70% Total Stock Market Index Fund, 20% International Stock Index Fund, and 10% Bond Index Fund. Rebalance once per year. That's it. This simple mix has historically returned 8-10% annually, while most people overthinking their "strategy" get 3-4%.

Your investment plan matters more than the actual assets.

When you start saving and investing, you must carefully consider all the options and plan the next steps. First, determine what you value more — convenience or control. Automatic assets are the best options for people who value their time more. Those who love to gamble and manage their money will prefer following the market and trade. Choose what works for you. Only afterward can you select where to invest.

To sum up, the fastest and most effective way to start building wealth lies in choosing your investing style and creating your first asset. It’s easier than it seems. Simply draw up a step-by-step investment plan and follow it no matter what.

chapters.title: Conclusion
chapters.text:
No matter your income level, you can create a system that will make you wealthy. You don’t have to be a broker or have a financial degree to change your life. The techniques are unconventional, but they’re proven to work. Describe what a rich life means to you and introduce new financial habits, starting with practicing negotiations to get better deals, analyzing your expenses, and reconsidering your credit cards.

Life is not just about investing more and saving more. There must be an apparent reason for the extra credit you want to get. Once the cause is clear, you can accumulate more and move faster toward your financial goals.

Finally, ignore the noise of money advice. Just ensure you follow these steps:

• Sort out your credit cards.
• Start high-interest savings and checking accounts.
• Open a 401(k) and investment account.
• Track your spending.
• Automate your transactions.
• Learn more about investments.

These six steps create your personal money-making machine that works 24/7. While your friends stress about money, you'll be watching your wealth grow automatically. Moreover, this system gets stronger over time: your money makes money, which makes more money. That's how ordinary people become millionaires.

Try this

To change your financial situation, try these steps:

• Outline plans that you have for each period of your life. You can write down goals for three, five, or ten years ahead of time.
• Describe who you want to be and what you want to own. For example, you may want to rent a lovely apartment in three years but own a beautiful house in ten years. Be realistic about it, but maintain hope.

Read it till the end?
